claims.claim: Excited about onchain privacy (ZK/MPC-based confidentiality) as the most underrated unlock and a non-negotiable for institutions/mainstream adoption
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about ZK proofs / zkVMs (SNARKs, Jolt) as foundational verifiable-compute infrastructure on a Moore's-law curve
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about stablecoins & payments (clear PMF, x402, lower fees; remaining problem is UX/rails)
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about proof of personhood / private unique identity as essential for a trustworthy internet in the AI era
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about AI x crypto (agent wallets, agent identity/payments, decentralized AI as counterweight to centralized AI)
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about fully onchain games as high-throughput, composable, autonomous worlds
claims.label: absent

claims.claim: Excited about decentralized social networks (open, user-owned social/identity that resists recentralization)
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Concerned about memecoins: not technically interesting, largely zero-sum, reputationally damaging to crypto
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Concerned about company-backed tokens mislabeled as network tokens (securities-like trust dependencies; a pie chart is not a protocol)
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about ETH as the clearest network token with automatic value capture via EIP-1559 burn; keeps pushing Ethereum to scale
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about World / World ID (WLD) as the most promising private, unique root of trust for proof-of-personhood
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Long-horizon structural concern about BTC: ossification limits programmability and the security model needs to change
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Neutral on market risk regime: ignores risk-on/risk-off herd cycle, trusts the price-innovation cycle (prices lag fundamentals); builder's optimism, not market timing
claims.label: confirmed

missed_changes: Introduced/championed 'arcade tokens' as a new, underappreciated token primitive (a fresh token taxonomy) — not anticipated by the prediction

Became vocal about network-token revenue capture via buyback-and-burn and the legal DUNA/DUNI wrappers (esp. Uniswap/UNI becoming a 'closed loop'); a newly prominent, concrete extension of the ETH/network-token thesis
Strong positive/analytical stance on perpetual-futures DEXes — calls them highly engineered, working better than ever, and credits them for the memecoin decline
Launched an emphatic wallet-security stance against blind signing — demanding clear signing on hardware wallets and threatening to publicly shame wallets that can't
Advocated new safety mechanisms: ERC-20R reversible transfers/asset recovery, transfer limits, and rate-limited minting/withdrawals + admin time-delays for vaults/bridges
Criticized passkeys' lack of portability/self-custody as an unacceptable bug for anything holding real money
Notable enthusiasm for AI-assisted coding / 'agentic engineering' (declaring vibe-coding over, testing agent frameworks) as a new prominent interest
New positive stance on RWA tokenization — personally bought PaxG gold, finds private credit onchain compelling, and views tokenized equities as a big plus
notes: Nearly every predicted stance is directly reaffirmed in the window, often emphatically (privacy: 'I will scream it forever'; Jolt: the Feb-2026 LayerZero/Zero launch; x402; World app usage; price-innovation cycle restated verbatim on 2025-11-17). No contradictions. ETH is confirmed at stance level (EVM-investment advocacy + burn-based network-token model) rather than via a standalone ETH-price/design statement. The company-backed-token concern manifests as boundary-policing/taxonomy work (defining network tokens vs company-backed/security tokens, 'protocols aren't corporations') rather than explicit warning. BTC concern is confirmed on the ossification/programmability dimension; the specific fee-vs-subsidy security-budget point isn't directly restated (a quantum-vulnerability point appears instead). Only 'fully onchain games' is genuinely absent. Several substantive new stances (arcade tokens, clear-signing campaign, ERC-20R, passkey portability, AI coding, RWAs) emerged that the prediction did not anticipate.
